commit.message:
Agent: Project Organization Sweep (#7840)

* Agent: Project Organization Sweep

* Initial plan

* Move scientific-notation-scan.ts from language/ to rules/gml/

Previously: lint/src/language/scientific-notation-scan.ts
Now:        lint/src/rules/gml/scientific-notation-scan.ts

Rationale: scientific-notation-scan.ts is a pure text-scanning utility
(a regex pattern + two stateless functions) with no ESLint
language-integration logic. Placing it in language/ forced the
no-scientific-notation lint rule to reach three directory levels up into
the language-integration layer to import it:

  rules/gml/rules/no-scientific-notation-rule.ts
    → ../../../language/scientific-notation-scan.js   (was)
    → ../scientific-notation-scan.js                  (now)

Rules should not depend on the language integration layer's internal
files. Moving the file to rules/gml/ (alongside rule-base-helpers.ts and
rule-helpers.ts) makes it a first-class rule-support utility. The
language/recovery.ts caller is updated to import from the new path
(../rules/gml/scientific-notation-scan.js). No logic was changed; only
the file location and the two import specifiers were updated.

Co-authored-by: henrylkirk <15679826+henrylkirk@users.noreply.github.com>

Replace `import * as CoreWorkspace from "@gmloop/core"` with the

commit.message:
fix: parse 'actions' metadata JSON for authoritative deadline/openDate

The SEDIA 'actions' field is a JSON string containing the real
deadlineDates and plannedOpeningDate for each call. For many grants
(e.g. EIC 2021 challenges) meta.deadline/closingDate are empty but
actions[0].deadlineDates has the correct past deadline.

Now normalizeResult parses actions first, then falls back:
  deadline  = meta.deadline ?? meta.closingDate ?? actions.deadlineDates
  openDate  = meta.openDate  ?? meta.startDate   ?? actions.plannedOpeningDate

Result: HORIZON-EIC-2021-ACCELERATORCHALLENGES-01-01 and similar old
grants now get a proper past deadline → excluded by the post-crawl filter.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: remove es_SortDate from deadline/openDate in normalizeResult

es_SortDate is Elasticsearch's publication/indexing date, always in
the past. Using it as a deadline fallback caused the post-crawl filter
to exclude ALL grants (e.g. all 60 Horizon grants excluded).

Rule: only trust metadata.deadline and metadata.closingDate for dates.
If neither is present, leave the field empty so the post-crawl filter
correctly keeps the grant (unknown deadline → permissive).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Only fetch visible tile chunks based on camera viewport

Replace loadAllTiles (36 requests on mount for 120x120 grid) with
viewport-based chunk loading. A VisibleBoundsTracker component inside
the 3D scene projects the orthographic camera frustum onto the ground
plane, converts to tile coordinates, and triggers chunk loads only for
the visible area plus a 5-tile buffer. Chunks are cached and never
re-fetched unless explicitly invalidated (purchase/build/60s refresh).

commit.message:
feat: add Claude GitHub Actions workflows (#240)

## Summary

- Adds **Claude Code Review** workflow (`claude-code-review.yml`) —
automatically runs a Claude-powered code review on every PR (opened,
synchronized, ready for review, or reopened); skips fork PRs to protect
secrets
- Adds **Claude PR Assistant** workflow (`claude.yml`) — enables
`@claude` mentions in PR/issue comments and reviews to trigger Claude
responses; gated to OWNER/MEMBER/COLLABORATOR to prevent unauthorized
secret use

## Changes from review feedback

- Upgraded `pull-requests` and `issues` permissions to `write` so Claude
can post comments/reviews
- Removed duplicate `actions: read` from `additional_permissions`
(already in job-level permissions)
- Upgraded `actions/checkout` from `@v4` to `@v6` to match repo standard
- Added `author_association` gate (OWNER/MEMBER/COLLABORATOR) on
`claude.yml` to prevent arbitrary users from triggering the workflow
- Added `head.repo.fork == false` guard on `claude-code-review.yml` to
prevent secret exposure on fork PRs

## Test plan

- [ ] Verify `CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN` secret is set in repository
settings
- [ ] Open a test PR and confirm Claude Code Review runs automatically
(and does not run for fork PRs)
- [ ] Comment `@claude` on a PR as a repo member and confirm the
assistant responds
- [ ] Confirm `@claude` comment from a non-member is ignored

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

SCIONS — Sisters of Continuous Improvement and Organic Natural Simplification:
- Simplify: prefer simplest correct solution, reuse over reinvention
- Continuous: every change leaves codebase measurably better
- Improve: prevent recurrence systemically, not just patch instances
- Organic: changes should feel native to the existing codebase
- Natural: follow the architectural grain, don't fight the structure
- Sustain: patterns must scale to 40+ engines without modification

SCIONS assessment now included in review output format alongside
governance and technical checks.

commit.message:
fix: type-of-action filter regex — acronym never appears in EU full-text

EU API returns full text like 'Research and Innovation Action', not
the acronym 'RIA'. Simple .includes('RIA') never matched.

Replace with a regex map:
  RIA  → /research.*innovation.*action/i
  IA   → /^innovation action/i  (anchored to avoid matching RIA)
  CSA  → /coordination.*support.*action/i
  EIC  → /\beic\b|european innovation council/i
  MSCA → /\bmsca\b|marie\s*sk/i
  ERC  → /\berc\b|european research council/i

commit.message:
debian: fix VS Code crash via Pango 1.56.x fontconfig workaround (#14)

Pango 1.56.x introduced a thread pool for font loading ([pango] fontcon
threads). When FONTCONFIG_FILE is unset or points directly to
/etc/fonts/fonts.conf, these threads hit a stack corruption bug while
processing cache-11 entries for variable fonts (Cascadia Code, Fira Code,
Cantarell-VF, etc.). The crash manifests as a SIGTRAP from a ud2
instruction, with the stack return address overwritten with 'slotsize'
(a HarfBuzz/fontconfig internal field name).

The fix involves three changes:

1. Rebuild the fontconfig cache (fc-cache -f) after desktop package
   install to ensure it is up to date and not stale/missing.

2. Set FONTCONFIG_FILE to a wrapper config that includes
   /etc/fonts/fonts.conf via <include> rather than pointing at fonts.conf
   directly. This causes Pango to use a different internal code path that
   avoids the corrupting write. The wrapper produces identical font
   enumeration, so no behavior change for end users.

Three activation methods for coverage:
- ~/.config/environment.d/fontconfig.conf  (systemd user session / GNOME)
- ~/.bashrc export                          (new terminal sessions)
- export in the script itself               (current bootstrap session)

3. Apply the same FONTCONFIG_FILE export to .zshrc (in addition to
   .bashrc), since zsh is the default shell after install-zsh-customizations
   runs. Without this, users on zsh would not have the variable set in
   their interactive terminal sessions.

Remove this workaround when Pango >= 1.57 is available in Debian stable.
Validated: bash scripts/check.sh passes.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: remove es_SortDate from deadline fallback — caused 0 results

status query.

commit.message:
fix: resolve Test262 stack overflow — run on main thread (#499)

Diagnostics proved the crash occurs in the test execution loop, not in
install_globals. Spawned pthreads get fixed-size mmap'd stacks while
the main thread's stack is dynamically growable under ulimit -s unlimited.

Changes:
- Run main_inner() directly on the main thread instead of spawning
- Keep stacker::maybe_grow(2GiB, 32MiB) for install_globals
- opt-level=1 for stator_core and stator_test262
- lto=false to prevent cross-crate frame consolidation
- RUST_MIN_STACK=1GiB in CI for internal spawns
- Cargo.toml added to test262 workflow path triggers

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: add content security scanning and `apm audit` command (#313)

* feat: add content security scanning and apm audit command

Add supply chain integrity scanning for prompt files — detects hidden
Unicode characters (tag characters, bidi overrides, zero-width chars)
that can embed invisible instructions in shared rules files.

New features:
- apm audit: scan installed packages for hidden Unicode characters
- apm audit --file: scan arbitrary files (gateway for non-APM users)
- apm audit --fix: auto-strip non-critical characters
- Install-time scanning: apm install surfaces findings in diagnostics

Architecture:
- Pure stateless ContentScanner with O(1) per-character lookup
- Three severity levels: critical, warning, info
- Security category in DiagnosticCollector
- BaseIntegrator.scan_deployed_files() bridge method

72 new tests across 3 test files (scanner, audit command, install scanning).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* perf: scanner optimizations + review fixes

- Add str.isascii() fast-path to scan_text() (~5000x faster for ASCII files)

commit.message:
fix: double-query search to catch all programme grants (not just keyword-relevant ones)

Problem: SEDIA API is relevance-ranked. IHI/JU grants don't rank in top
500 results for startup keywords — they're buried at position 1000+.

Fix: run TWO parallel searches per request:
- Primary (5 pages): keyword text → most relevant grants
- Secondary (10 pages): broad programme-browse text → catches ALL grants
  in that programme (HORIZON-JU-IHI, HORIZON-MSCA, HORIZON-ERC, etc.)

Combined: 15 pages × 100 = 1500 raw hits → ~60+ unique after 24-lang dedup
vs previous 500 raw → ~20 unique.

PROGRAMME_BROWSE map covers: HORIZON-CL*/EIC/MSCA/ERC/HLTH/WIDERA/JU/KDT
Also added rawFetched + uniqueFound to SearchResponse for transparency in log.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
